backend_independent_hit_costs.note: A cache HIT never invokes the LLM backend, so hit TTFT is backend-independent and applies to a llama.cpp-backed alias too.

ollama_path_fallback.origin: prior bestcfg-matrix-halo-b.json ollama-resident-p8 overlay == task-cited fallback

qwen_path_this_session.note: model:auto->fast_qa->qwen/qwen3.5-rocm->ollama(llama3.2:3b); miss inflated by ollama cold-load; hit costs same order as fallback

cache_overlay_taskb.fallback_used: ollama overlay: miss 1,041 ms -> exact-hit 689 ms (saved 351 ms), semantic-0.92-hit 659 ms (server-independent, task-sanctioned).

llamacpp_direct_latency_ms.note: direct llama-server 16-tok chat compute; the miss-side cost a llama.cpp-backed cache would pay (~0.6-0.9s), well under the ollama cold miss

llamacpp_via_router.note: identical to the prior authoritative bestcfg-matrix-halo-b.json llamacpp overlay (also 0% / null)

cache_overlay_taskb.objective: llama.cpp-specific semantic-cache repeat-TTFT (miss -> exact-repeat-hit -> semantic-0.92-hit) through the router path
cache_overlay_taskb.root_cause: The router's model->backend binding egresses to a FIXED Envoy cluster keyed by backend_ref name (ollama_local -> ollama:11434, llm-katan -> llm-katan:8000); there is no llama-server cluster. repoint_backend.py rewrites the endpoint STRING in runtime-config.yaml and the router hot-reloads (config_reloaded fires), but traffic still goes to the ollama cluster. With the alias's external model id set to 'ggml-org/gpt-oss-120b-GGUF' (which ollama has not pulled) every miss returns HTTP 404 not_found from ollama, so no completion is produced or cached -> 0% hits / null TTFT. llama.cpp itself IGNORES the OpenAI model field (verified: bogus model -> HTTP 200) and serves gpt-oss-120b correctly when called directly.
cache_overlay_taskb.router_health: no OOM (exit 137); router stayed up (exit=0) through all repoint/overlay/restore cycles; config hash held at a78aebc5fd5f...a244cf; every run restored the original runtime-config (no leftover repoint/cache/threshold injection).

known_reference.note: Prior known points on the winning config (for cross-check).

observations: Aggregate is nearly flat from --parallel 1 to 2 (50.3 -> 51.0 tok/s, +1.2%): the MXFP4 120B is memory-bandwidth-bound, so concurrent streams split bandwidth (~24.0 tok/s each at c2). Real aggregate gain only appears at --parallel 8 (79.1 tok/s @ c8) but that violates the latency gate (p95 3742.1 ms).

Single-stream decode @ c1 falls from 52.1 tok/s (--parallel 1) to 32.7 tok/s (--parallel 8): raising --parallel adds llama.cpp slot/batch overhead that penalizes even a lone request, so over-provisioning slots hurts the latency-critical single-user path.
sweet_spot.rationale: --parallel 2 is the largest slot count whose TTFT p95 @ c2 (1044.7 ms) holds the <= 2000 ms interactive gate (51.0 tok/s aggregate, ~24.0 tok/s per stream). --parallel 4 is where it breaks (TTFT p95 3150.7 ms > gate).
